title: Stock Market Timing Mistakes That Investors Can't Afford to Make | Dimensional
gist: A short podcast landing page (Episode 49 of The Informed Investor) on why market timing is alluring. The text is only a teaser with three key takeaways, not the episode content; emitted as an honest light card based solely on the listed takeaways.

insights.claim: Dimensional asserts that long-term investment outcomes are driven by time in the market rather than attempts to time the market.
insights.speaker: Mark Gochnour, Head of Global Client Services Group
insights.so_what: Reinforces a buy-and-hold discipline; the cost of missing the best market days argues against tactical exits.
insights.quote: What matters is time in the market, not timing the market.

insights.claim: Dimensional recommends sticking with a diversified, low-cost portfolio and avoiding emotional decision-making as the antidote to the temptation of market timing.
insights.speaker: Mark Gochnour, Head of Global Client Services Group
insights.so_what: Behavioral guardrail: structure (diversification, low cost) plus emotional discipline preserves returns better than timing bets.
insights.quote: Stick with a diversified, low-cost portfolio. Avoid emotional decision-making with your investments.

source_meta.author: Featuring Mark Gochnour, Head of Global Client Services Group
